name: Immer
what_it_does: JavaScript library that lets you work with immutable state using mutable syntax. The produce() function creates a draft of the current state where you can apply direct mutations — Immer tracks changes and produces a new immutable state using structural sharing. Used extensively in Redux Toolkit (built-in), React state management, and anywhere immutable data structures need readable mutation syntax. Eliminates the need for spread operators and nested immutable updates.
use_cases: Update nested React or Redux state using direct mutations inside produce() instead of spreading nested objects
Simplify complex state updates in Redux reducers — Redux Toolkit uses Immer internally for all slice reducers
Create immutable updates for complex data structures (arrays of objects, deeply nested state) with readable code
Use useImmer hook as a drop-in replacement for useState that enables mutable syntax for complex state
Implement structural sharing in custom state managers where only changed nodes are replaced (not the entire tree)
not_for: Performance-critical code paths with very large objects — Immer's proxy overhead adds cost for massive data structures
Simple flat state — spreading a flat object is as readable as Immer without the library overhead
Non-JavaScript environments — Immer is JavaScript/TypeScript only
best_when: You have deeply nested state that's painful to update immutably with spreads — Immer makes nested updates as readable as mutations.
avoid_when: Your state is simple (1-2 levels deep) — spread operators are sufficient without Immer's overhead for flat state.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Completely free. Created by Michel Weststrate (MobX creator).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Immer throws descriptive errors for common mistakes (returning new state from produce AND mutating draft, trying to use draft outside produce). TypeScript types infer correctly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: produce() is deterministic — same base state + same recipe function = same output state.
known_agent_gotchas: Cannot both mutate the draft AND return a new value from produce() — must either mutate (void return) or return a new value, not both
Cannot use async functions directly in produce() — use produceWithPatches for async state updates or restructure async logic
Proxy-based drafts cannot be passed to non-Immer-aware code that expects plain objects — finalize the draft first with produce()
Class instances in state require enabling setAutoFreeze(false) — Immer freezes state by default, breaking class instances with methods
Map and Set are supported but require special handling — Immer handles them but behavior differs from plain objects
Performance with very large arrays (10k+ items) can be slower than direct immutable updates — profile before using in hot paths

security_score_components.security_notes: In-memory state utility — no network calls. No security concerns. MIT licensed. State freezing prevents accidental mutations from external code.
